reviewer_objection: Why is the multilingual package not yet a cross-country comparison table if it already has six non-English rows?
bounded_response_now: Because the current package is still an evidence-maturity ladder, not a country-comparable denominator: only 1 row is direct official and substantive, 2 rows are bounded non-official comparators, 3 rows remain queue-only limitations, and all 6 still require translation-review metadata.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: preserves the 1/2/3 split, translation burden, and non-ranking ceiling.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Table 1D legend note; Figure 6 methods caption; thesis claim-support map.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep every multilingual reuse tied to Table 1D / CCR-006 and do not flatten rows into country scores.

reviewer_objection: Why keep Lithuania and Estonia visible at all if both are still unresolved and queue-only?
bounded_response_now: Because the unresolved high-undercovered band is itself publishable negative evidence about coverage bias, rights/access friction, and challenge-gated public visibility. Hiding those rows would overstate multilingual capture success and erase the fact that the current high-undercovered band still has zero resolved rows.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: unresolved rare-language rows remain limitations/coverage-pressure signals only.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Queue-depth bar; blocker-class matrix; high-undercovered map layer.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Preserve Lithuania and Estonia only in queue/limitations views until a genuinely different compliant public route appears.

reviewer_objection: Why is Finland not promoted into a substantive multilingual geography if one Finnish row is already direct-source resolved?
bounded_response_now: Because Finland currently combines one bounded broadcaster-source procedural-contamination row with one unresolved deepfake-abuse queue row, and the resolved row still lacks direct official phenomenon-confirming corroboration. That makes Finland a mixed caution geography, not a stable substantive package comparable to the Slovenian official row.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: keeps Finland below default substantive map/table status.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Figure 6 caution layer; procedural-contamination comparator note; authority-upgrade funnel.
next_smallest_publishability_move: At most one quick official police, court, or ministry corroboration route; otherwise keep Finland bounded and mixed.

reviewer_objection: Why is Slovenia allowed as the only default substantive multilingual exemplar if machine translation is still involved?
bounded_response_now: Because Slovenia is the only row with direct official source support, but even that row remains bounded to project existence, stated AI-assisted border-management framing, and pilot-style wording. Translation caution remains mandatory and the row still does not support production deployment, legality, compliance, proportionality, or effectiveness claims.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: keeps ORION as a bounded official exemplar under translation and deployment ceilings.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Default Slovenia map/table badge; authority-upgrade funnel; border-governance appendix note.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Exploit now at the current official-project-page ceiling; only one extra companion-document pass if it materially sharpens pilot-versus-deployment wording.

reviewer_objection: Is the multilingual package methodologically weak because all six rows still require translation review?
bounded_response_now: It is methodologically bounded rather than unusable. The contribution is precisely that translation burden, authority class, and publication bucket are kept explicit row by row. The package is publishable as a multilingual evidence-governance demonstration, not as language-neutral validated fact extraction.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: translation caution remains first-class metadata across manuscript, dashboard, and appendix reuse.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Translation badge layer; reviewer appendix on evidence burden; dashboard legend QA.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep six-of-six translation-review-required metadata attached to every multilingual map, table, and prose derivative.

reviewer_objection: Is the multilingual package already publishable despite having only one direct-official row and two high-undercovered unresolved rows?
bounded_response_now: Yes, but only as a bounded methods/results and observability-bias package. Its article-ready value is that it shows how a public-source atlas can separate one direct-official exemplar, two bounded non-official comparators, and three queue-only rare-language limitations without overstating multilingual coverage.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: publishability is methodological and dashboard-oriented, not prevalence-oriented.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Table 1D reviewer note; Figure 6 legend sentence; thesis methods/results bridge.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Route the package into reviewer-facing methods/results prose and keep substantive expansion subordinate to one future compliant authority upgrade.
